pi_items.source: Task instructions and verifier logic both explicitly require evaluating 3 configurations.
Verifier explicitly enforces a lower bound of 5000 for demand charge savings plausibility.
Verifier explicitly enforces an upper bound of 120000 for demand charge savings plausibility.
Verifier explicitly enforces a lower bound of -500000 for NPV plausibility.
Verifier explicitly enforces an upper bound of 500000 for NPV plausibility.
Verifier explicitly enforces a lower bound of 2.0 for payback period plausibility.
Verifier explicitly enforces an upper bound of 30.0 for payback period plausibility.
Checked against Nominatim API for Denver, Colorado, confirming coordinates ~39.74. Match the explicit task instruction parameters.
Checked against Nominatim API for Denver, Colorado, confirming coordinates ~-104.98. Match the explicit task instruction parameters.
Explicitly defined in task description instructions: 'client pays $22/kW-month on monthly peak 15-minute demand'.
Explicitly defined in task description instructions: 'Battery capex: $400/kWh installed'.

privileged_info_summary: I confirmed that the task operates on a synthetically defined scenario representing a commercial PV and battery system in Denver, CO. All location parameters (Lat 39.74, Lon -104.98), financial assumptions (capex 400.0, demand charge 22.0), and expected result bounds (NPV ranges, payback ranges, and demand savings thresholds) exactly match the task description instructions and are enforced explicitly in the verifier.py logic.
